title: Hieracium subpanduratum
taxonomy: Hieracium subpanduratum is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a member of the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is divided into many species, with Hieracium subpanduratum being one of the several species within this group. The classification of Hieracium subpanduratum can be further broken down into its binomial nomenclature, consisting of the genus name Hieracium and the species name subpanduratum. This classification system helps to identify and organize the various species within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium subpanduratum, also known as sub-pandurate hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subpanduratum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium subpanduratum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and wounds.
